name: American Football
description:
'''American Football''' is an American [[Midwest emo|midwest emo]] band from [[Urbana, Illinois]], originally active from 1997 to 2000. They reformed in 2014.

The band was formed by guitarist, bassist and singer [[Mike Kinsella]] (formerly of [[Cap'n Jazz]] and [[Joan of Arc (band)|Joan of Arc]]; currently of [[Owen (band)|Owen]]), guitarist Steve Holmes (also a member of the Geese), and drummer and trumpet player Steve Lamos (formerly of the One Up Downstairs, one-time member of [[the Firebird Band]] and Edward Burch & the Staunch Characters, and currently of the Geese and DMS). Lamos announced his departure from the band in 2021 but rejoined in 2023.

Despite the group's short initial lifespan, their [[American Football (1999 album)|self-titled debut album]] became one of the most acclaimed [[emo]] and [[math rock]] records of its era. After reuniting in 2014, with Kinsella's cousin [[Nate Kinsella]] joining the band, American Football has released three more albums, all bearing the same name as their debut: ''[[American Football (2016 album)|American Football]]'' (2016), ''[[American Football (2019 album)|American Football]]'' (2019), and ''[[American Football (2026 album)|American Football]]'' (2026).
